text: All mammals are warm-blooded. All whales are mammals. Therefore, are whales warm-blooded?
If A is taller than B, and B is taller than C, and C is taller than D, who is the shortest?
A bag contains 3 red marbles and 7 blue marbles. What is the probability of drawing two red marbles in a row without replacement?
The Barber of Seville shaves all men who do not shave themselves. Who shaves the barber?
Given sets A = {1,2,3,4} and B = {3,4,5,6}, what is the cardinality of A union B minus A intersection B?
You have a 3-liter jug and a 5-liter jug with no markings. How do you measure exactly 4 liters of water?
Prove that the sum of any two even integers is always even.
No fish are mammals. Some aquatic animals are fish. Can we conclude that some aquatic animals are not mammals?
If it rains, the ground gets wet. The ground is wet. Does it necessarily follow that it rained?
A traveler reaches a fork with two guards: one always lies, one always tells truth. With one question, which path leads to safety?
In a group of 23 people, what is the probability that at least two share a birthday? Is it above or below 50%?
If all A are B, and some B are C, can we conclude that some A are C?
A statement is true if and only if it is provable. Is this statement itself true, provable, both, or neither?
You have 12 coins, one of which is counterfeit and slightly heavier. Using a balance scale three times, identify the counterfeit coin.
If P implies Q, and Q implies R, and R is false, what can you conclude about P?
How many distinct ways can you arrange the letters in the word MISSISSIPPI?
Every integer greater than 1 is either prime or a product of primes. Is 1 prime? Justify your answer.
There are 100 prisoners numbered 1-100. Each must find their number in one of 100 boxes. They may open 50 boxes. What strategy maximizes survival?
If the negation of a statement is false, what is the truth value of the original statement?
A set contains all sets that do not contain themselves. Does this set contain itself?
